sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI ITRA 164 of 2018 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on office objection No.1. 2. For orders on CMA No.195/2018. 3. For hearing of main case. 21.11.2025 Mr. Muhammad Aqeel Qureshi, advocate for the applicant. Matter is pending since 2018 without any progress. Even notice has not been sought / issued till date. On 12.09.2023, following order was passed: “12.09.2023 Mr. Muhammad Aqeel Qureshi, Advocate for Applicant. 1. To be complied with before the next date. 2. Granted subject to all just exceptions. 3. Before notice could be ordered, learned Counsel for the Applicant is directed to seek instructions as to pendency of any Reference Application against order of the Appellate Tribunal Inland Revenue in ITA No. 388/KB/2014 Tax year 2012 dated 07.10.2016. Adjourned.” Even today two years later, counsel for the applicant is present and unable to assist. It appears that the applicant is not interested in the present proceedings. Accordingly, in view of judgment of CIR vs. Rafeh Limited reported as PLD 2020 SC 518, the reference application is dismissed for non-prosecution. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Judge Judge Khuhro/PS
